metadata.title: Define a Reusable Skill for Common Teacher Workflows with Claude Code

beats.narration: A grading skill you write once this semester runs every submission cycle forever — including for the teacher down the hall who has never written a prompt.
beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear.

beats.narration: A skill is the difference between a workflow you run once and a workflow you run every semester. The definition is written once, invoked by name by anyone, and constrained the same way every time.
Ask Claude to write a Claude Code skill definition for a grade pattern analyzer — no grades in output, YAML frontmatter, typed parameters.

props.command:
claude "Write Claude Code Skill definition:
  .claude/skills/grade-pattern-analyzer.md
  inputs: submissions_dir, rubric.md
  scan each submission for rubric criteria
  output per-student report:
    strength flags, gap flags,
    recommended_focus sentence
    NO numeric or letter grade
  YAML frontmatter: name, description, params"

beats.narration: The skill definition: YAML frontmatter with name, description, and typed parameters. The workflow scans submissions against rubric criteria and outputs strengths, gaps, and a recommended focus sentence per student. The grade exclusion is explicit in the definition.

props.code:
---
name: grade-pattern-analyzer
description: Scans student submissions against
  rubric criteria. Reports patterns — no grades.
parameters:
  submissions_dir: {type: string}
  rubric_file:     {type: string}
---

# Grade Pattern Analyzer

For each file in submissions_dir:
1. Read submission
2. For each criterion in rubric_file:
   flag STRENGTH if criterion met
   flag GAP if criterion missing
3. Output per student:
   strengths: [criterion list]
   gaps: [criterion list]
   recommended_focus: <one sentence>
   !! NO grades — numeric or letter !!

beats.narration: Skill invoked: /grade-pattern-analyzer submissions/ rubric.md. Three submissions processed. Per-student report for each: two strengths, one gap, one recommended focus sentence. No grades in any output. The skill runs the same way every time — the teacher does not re-specify the workflow or the grade-exclusion constraint.
Add a second feedback-drafter skill and chain the two skills together.

props.command:
claude "Add second skill: feedback-drafter.
  Takes pattern-analyzer output.
  Drafts per-student feedback sentences.
  Chain the two skills — verify workflow runs
  without re-specifying either."

beats.narration: Chain run: pattern-analyzer runs first, outputs structured report. Feedback-drafter takes the report as input, drafts one paragraph per student from the flags and recommended focus. The full workflow runs with two skill invocations. Neither skill was re-specified — the definitions in .claude/skills/ contain the full workflow.

A skill definition is three things: a YAML frontmatter with parameter types, a workflow description without grades, and an explicit exclusion constraint. Write it once, share the file with the teacher down the hall, invoke it every semester.
Next: map your build decisions to what students are reading in their textbook.
